title: Americans are freaking out over China's open-source AI strategy
excerpt: The latest AI model out of China is causing another meltdown in Silicon Valley, highlighting the divide over the right way to run the AI race.
content_html:
An OpenAI exec's comments on China's Kimi K3 kicked off a big US tech debate

By Peter Gelling

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Every so often, a Chinese company releases a new AI model, and Americans freak out.

That's what happened last week when China's Moonshot AI debuted Kimi K3, which crushed a bunch of notable benchmarks. By most accounts, Kimi K3 rivals some leading US models at a fraction of the expense.

The new model triggered another round of panic that China is closing in on the US in the race to corner the AI market and accusations that the Chinese are training their models on the backs of the work already done by Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google. (Earlier this month, Business Insider's Ali Barr highlighted the irony in that accusation.)

Increasingly, the tension boils down to a distinct strategy divide between the two countries: The Chinese have embraced open-source or open-weight models, which allow developers to inspect and customize them, while the US mostly remains closed.

Debate over open or closed models

A debate erupted on X over the weekend after an OpenAI executive published a lengthy reaction to Kimi K3.

"I am personally surprised the Chinese state continues to allow the open sourcing of models this good, given potential risks," Dean Ball, a former senior advisor on AI to President Donald Trump who is newly OpenAI's head of strategy, wrote on X.

He said the open-weight strategy would lead to full "AI communism" and that open models can be "decelerationist" because they "deter AI capex."

What really got the conversation going, though, was this:

"I would guess that the Trump Administration will at some point realize that their best strategy here would be to create large amounts of regulatory risk around the use of open-weight Chinese models," he wrote.

Ball suggested that manufacturing fear, uncertainty, and doubt — or "FUD," for those in the know — in the regulatory process would cause most American companies to avoid using open models.

He later clarified that this was his prediction, not a recommendation, and that he supports open-source up until the point AI becomes too dangerous, which he said would be a "sad day."

The response was swift and widespread.

Fabricating regulatory confusion in support of American AI labs sounds a lot like "regulatory capture." That's when government agencies tasked with regulating an industry design rules to support it, often on the advice of industry insiders themselves.

Anthropic and OpenAI have maintained that their models are too powerful to be open — that doing so would be dangerous, allowing anyone to wield their tools for any end, with little oversight. A closed system gives the maker greater overall control, including over security, access, and pricing. The labs have warned that the open-weight models coming out of China are a threat to national security and to their businesses.

David Sacks, a venture capitalist who served as Trump's first AI and crypto czar before moving in March to cochair the president's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology, said the "weaponization of regulatory uncertainty" was "completely unacceptable."

"We are at a critical inflection point in AI policy. The leading closed labs, already a duopoly in terms of AI model revenue, want the government to eliminate their open source competition," he wrote on X in response to Ball's post. That "duopoly" refers to OpenAI and Anthropic. "They have laid their cards on the table. It is time for the rest of Silicon Valley — the vast majority that still values open competition — to do the same."

Sacks' "All-In" podcast cohost and fellow VC, Chamath Palihapitiya, was equally direct: "The future is open source," he wrote on X. "We need to embrace it and get on with it."

Suhail Doshi, a prominent software engineer and entrepreneur, said American AI labs trained their products on "humanity's data and didn't pay a cent."

"Any lobbying or legislation that calls for banning open weight models in the name of 'distillation' is total BS," he wrote on X. "This is a fight against future American innovation."

A Citrini Research analyst who goes by Jukan on X disagreed with Ball and the warnings about a Chinese takeover, writing that open source models don't automatically position companies to dominate. He said DeepSeek, for example, can operate more efficiently — which keeps token costs lower — because of its proprietary operations, not just its open-source framework.

"Chinese companies may lack sufficient compute capacity to serve all the inference demand themselves, but they are not selling at a loss or failing to recoup their training costs," Jukan wrote.

Read next

Peter Gelling

Peter Gelling is a Senior Editor and the Weekend Bureau Chief at Business Insider. He also still writes, mostly about the AI industry, universal basic income, the economy, campaign finance reform, geopolitics, and anything else that inspires him.He was previously the Geopolitics Editor at Quartz and a Senior Editor at GlobalPost. From 2005 to 2010, he was a correspondent for The New York Times based in Jakarta, Indonesia.

sentences.en: Moonshot AI's latest model reignited the open versus closed-source debate.
sentences.zh: Moonshot AI 的最新模型重新引发了开源与闭源的争论。

sentences.en: HECTOR RETAMAL / AFP via Getty Images
sentences.zh: 赫克托·雷塔马尔/法新社/盖蒂图片社

blocks.html_en: Every so often, a Chinese company releases a new AI model, and Americans freak out.

sentences.en: Every so often, a Chinese company releases a new AI model, and Americans freak out.
sentences.zh: 每隔一段时间，一家中国公司发布一个新的人工智能模型，就会引起美国人的恐慌。

blocks.html_en: That's what happened last week when China's Moonshot AI debuted Kimi K3, which crushed a bunch of notable benchmarks. By most accounts, Kimi K3 rivals some leading US models at a fraction of the expense.

sentences.en: That's what happened last week when China's Moonshot AI debuted Kimi K3, which crushed a bunch of notable benchmarks.
sentences.zh: 这就是上周中国的 Moonshot AI 推出 Kimi K3 时发生的事情，它打破了一系列值得注意的基准。

sentences.en: By most accounts, Kimi K3 rivals some leading US models at a fraction of the expense.
sentences.zh: 大多数人认为，Kimi K3 可以与美国一些领先的型号相媲美，而价格却只是它的一小部分。

blocks.html_en: The new model triggered another round of panic that China is closing in on the US in the race to corner the AI market and accusations that the Chinese are training their models on the backs of the work already done by Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google. (Earlier this month, Business Insider's Ali Barr highlighted the irony in that accusation.)

sentences.en: The new model triggered another round of panic that China is closing in on the US in the race to corner the AI market and accusations that the Chinese are training their models on the backs of the work already done by Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google.
sentences.zh: 新模型引发了另一轮恐慌，认为中国在垄断人工智能市场的竞赛中正在逼近美国，并指责中国人在 Anthropic、OpenAI 和谷歌已经完成的工作的基础上训练他们的模型。

sentences.en: (Earlier this month, Business Insider's Ali Barr highlighted the irony in that accusation.)
sentences.zh: （本月早些时候，《商业内幕》的阿里·巴尔强调了这一指控的讽刺意味。）

blocks.html_en: Increasingly, the tension boils down to a distinct strategy divide between the two countries: The Chinese have embraced open-source or open-weight models, which allow developers to inspect and customize them, while the US mostly remains closed.

sentences.en: Increasingly, the tension boils down to a distinct strategy divide between the two countries: The Chinese have embraced open-source or open-weight models, which allow developers to inspect and customize them, while the US mostly remains closed.
sentences.zh: 紧张局势逐渐归结为两国之间明显的战略分歧：中国接受开源或开放权重模型，允许开发人员检查和定制它们，而美国则大多保持封闭。

blocks.html_en: Debate over open or closed models

sentences.en: Debate over open or closed models
sentences.zh: 关于开放或封闭模型的争论

blocks.html_en: A debate erupted on X over the weekend after an OpenAI executive published a lengthy reaction to Kimi K3.

sentences.en: A debate erupted on X over the weekend after an OpenAI executive published a lengthy reaction to Kimi K3.
sentences.zh: 周末，一位 OpenAI 高管发表了对 Kimi K3 的长篇回应后，X 上爆发了一场争论。

blocks.html_en: "I am personally surprised the Chinese state continues to allow the open sourcing of models this good, given potential risks," Dean Ball, a former senior advisor on AI to President Donald Trump who is newly OpenAI's head of strategy, wrote on X.

sentences.en: "I am personally surprised the Chinese state continues to allow the open sourcing of models this good, given potential risks," Dean Ball, a former senior advisor on AI to President Donald Trump who is newly OpenAI's head of strategy, wrote on X.
sentences.zh: 唐纳德·特朗普总统的前人工智能高级顾问、新任 OpenAI 战略主管迪恩·鲍尔 (Dean Ball) 在 X 上写道：“考虑到潜在的风险，我个人感到惊讶的是，中国政府继续允许如此好的模型开源。”

blocks.html_en: He said the open-weight strategy would lead to full "AI communism" and that open models can be "decelerationist" because they "deter AI capex."

sentences.en: He said the open-weight strategy would lead to full "AI communism" and that open models can be "decelerationist" because they "deter AI capex."
sentences.zh: 他表示，开放权重策略将导致全面的“人工智能共产主义”，而开放模型可能是“减速主义者”，因为它们“阻止了人工智能资本支出”。

blocks.html_en: What really got the conversation going, though, was this:

sentences.en: What really got the conversation going, though, was this:
sentences.zh: 不过，真正让谈话继续下去的是：

blocks.html_en: "I would guess that the Trump Administration will at some point realize that their best strategy here would be to create large amounts of regulatory risk around the use of open-weight Chinese models," he wrote.

sentences.en: "I would guess that the Trump Administration will at some point realize that their best strategy here would be to create large amounts of regulatory risk around the use of open-weight Chinese models," he wrote.
sentences.zh: 他写道：“我猜特朗普政府在某个时候会意识到，他们最好的策略是围绕开放权重中国模型的使用创造大量监管风险。”

blocks.html_en: Ball suggested that manufacturing fear, uncertainty, and doubt — or "FUD," for those in the know — in the regulatory process would cause most American companies to avoid using open models.

sentences.en: Ball suggested that manufacturing fear, uncertainty, and doubt — or "FUD," for those in the know — in the regulatory process would cause most American companies to avoid using open models.
sentences.zh: 鲍尔表示，监管过程中制造业的恐惧、不确定性和怀疑（对于知情人士来说是“FUD”）将导致大多数美国公司避免使用开放模式。

blocks.html_en: He later clarified that this was his prediction, not a recommendation, and that he supports open-source up until the point AI becomes too dangerous, which he said would be a "sad day."

sentences.en: He later clarified that this was his prediction, not a recommendation, and that he supports open-source up until the point AI becomes too dangerous, which he said would be a "sad day."
sentences.zh: 他后来澄清说，这是他的预测，而不是建议，并且他支持开源，直到人工智能变得太危险为止，他说这将是“悲伤的一天”。

blocks.html_en: The response was swift and widespread.

sentences.en: The response was swift and widespread.
sentences.zh: 反应迅速而广泛。

blocks.html_en: Fabricating regulatory confusion in support of American AI labs sounds a lot like "regulatory capture." That's when government agencies tasked with regulating an industry design rules to support it, often on the advice of industry insiders themselves.

sentences.en: Fabricating regulatory confusion in support of American AI labs sounds a lot like "regulatory capture."
sentences.zh: 为了支持美国人工智能实验室而制造监管混乱听起来很像“监管捕获”。

sentences.en: That's when government agencies tasked with regulating an industry design rules to support it, often on the advice of industry insiders themselves.
sentences.zh: 那时，政府机构的任务是规范行业设计规则以支持它，通常是根据业内人士自己的建议。

blocks.html_en: Anthropic and OpenAI have maintained that their models are too powerful to be open — that doing so would be dangerous, allowing anyone to wield their tools for any end, with little oversight. A closed system gives the maker greater overall control, including over security, access, and pricing. The labs have warned that the open-weight models coming out of China are a threat to national security and to their businesses.

sentences.en: Anthropic and OpenAI have maintained that their models are too powerful to be open — that doing so would be dangerous, allowing anyone to wield their tools for any end, with little oversight.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 和 OpenAI 坚称，他们的模型过于强大，无法开放——这样做是危险的，因为任何人都可以在几乎不受监督的情况下为任何目的使用他们的工具。

sentences.en: A closed system gives the maker greater overall control, including over security, access, and pricing.
sentences.zh: 封闭的系统为制造商提供了更大的整体控制力，包括安全性、访问权限和定价。

sentences.en: The labs have warned that the open-weight models coming out of China are a threat to national security and to their businesses.
sentences.zh: 这些实验室警告说，来自中国的开放重量模型对国家安全及其业务构成威胁。

blocks.html_en: David Sacks, a venture capitalist who served as Trump's first AI and crypto czar before moving in March to cochair the president's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology, said the "weaponization of regulatory uncertainty" was "completely unacceptable."

sentences.en: David Sacks, a venture capitalist who served as Trump's first AI and crypto czar before moving in March to cochair the president's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology, said the "weaponization of regulatory uncertainty" was "completely unacceptable."
sentences.zh: 风险投资家戴维·萨克斯 (David Sacks) 曾担任特朗普首任人工智能和加密货币沙皇，随后于 3 月份担任总统科学技术顾问委员会联合主席。他表示，“监管不确定性武器化”是“完全不可接受的”。

blocks.html_en: "We are at a critical inflection point in AI policy. The leading closed labs, already a duopoly in terms of AI model revenue, want the government to eliminate their open source competition," he wrote on X in response to Ball's post. That "duopoly" refers to OpenAI and Anthropic. "They have laid their cards on the table. It is time for the rest of Silicon Valley — the vast majority that still values open competition — to do the same."

sentences.en: "We are at a critical inflection point in AI policy.
sentences.zh: “我们正处于人工智能政策的关键转折点。

sentences.en: The leading closed labs, already a duopoly in terms of AI model revenue, want the government to eliminate their open source competition," he wrote on X in response to Ball's post.
sentences.zh: 领先的封闭实验室在人工智能模型收入方面已经形成双头垄断，他们希望政府消除他们的开源竞争，”他在 X 上回应 Ball 的帖子时写道。

sentences.en: That "duopoly" refers to OpenAI and Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 这个“双寡头”指的是 OpenAI 和 Anthropic。

sentences.en: "They have laid their cards on the table.
sentences.zh: “他们已经把牌摆在桌面上了。

sentences.en: It is time for the rest of Silicon Valley — the vast majority that still values open competition — to do the same."
sentences.zh: 现在是硅谷其他地方——绝大多数仍然重视公开竞争的地方——也应该这样做的时候了。”

blocks.html_en: Sacks' "All-In" podcast cohost and fellow VC, Chamath Palihapitiya, was equally direct: "The future is open source," he wrote on X. "We need to embrace it and get on with it."

sentences.en: Sacks' "All-In" podcast cohost and fellow VC, Chamath Palihapitiya, was equally direct: "The future is open source," he wrote on X.
sentences.zh: Sacks 的“All-In”播客联合主持人兼风投同事 Chamath Palihapitiya 同样直接：“未来是开源的，”他在 X 上写道。

sentences.en: "We need to embrace it and get on with it."
sentences.zh: “我们需要拥抱它并继续下去。”

blocks.html_en: Suhail Doshi, a prominent software engineer and entrepreneur, said American AI labs trained their products on "humanity's data and didn't pay a cent."

sentences.en: Suhail Doshi, a prominent software engineer and entrepreneur, said American AI labs trained their products on "humanity's data and didn't pay a cent."
sentences.zh: 著名软件工程师和企业家苏海尔·多西 (Suhail Doshi) 表示，美国人工智能实验室根据“人类数据来训练他们的产品，并且没有支付一分钱”。

blocks.html_en: "Any lobbying or legislation that calls for banning open weight models in the name of 'distillation' is total BS," he wrote on X. "This is a fight against future American innovation."

sentences.en: "Any lobbying or legislation that calls for banning open weight models in the name of 'distillation' is total BS," he wrote on X.
sentences.zh: “任何以‘蒸馏’的名义呼吁禁止开放重量模型的游说或立法都是胡说八道，”他在 X 上写道。

sentences.en: "This is a fight against future American innovation."
sentences.zh: “这是针对美国未来创新的一场斗争。”

blocks.html_en: A Citrini Research analyst who goes by Jukan on X disagreed with Ball and the warnings about a Chinese takeover, writing that open source models don't automatically position companies to dominate. He said DeepSeek, for example, can operate more efficiently — which keeps token costs lower — because of its proprietary operations, not just its open-source framework.

sentences.en: A Citrini Research analyst who goes by Jukan on X disagreed with Ball and the warnings about a Chinese takeover, writing that open source models don't automatically position companies to dominate.
sentences.zh: X 上的一位名为 Jukan 的 Citrini Research 分析师不同意 Ball 以及有关中国收购的警告，他写道，开源模式不会自动使公司占据主导地位。

sentences.en: He said DeepSeek, for example, can operate more efficiently — which keeps token costs lower — because of its proprietary operations, not just its open-source framework.
sentences.zh: 他说，例如，DeepSeek 可以更高效地运营，从而降低代币成本，因为它具有专有操作，而不仅仅是其开源框架。

blocks.html_en: "Chinese companies may lack sufficient compute capacity to serve all the inference demand themselves, but they are not selling at a loss or failing to recoup their training costs," Jukan wrote.

sentences.en: "Chinese companies may lack sufficient compute capacity to serve all the inference demand themselves, but they are not selling at a loss or failing to recoup their training costs," Jukan wrote.
sentences.zh: 朱坎写道：“中国公司可能缺乏足够的计算能力来满足所有推理需求，但他们不会亏本出售或无法收回培训成本。”

content_html_zh:
OpenAI 高管对中国 Kimi K3 的评论引发了一场美国科技大辩论

作者：Peter Gelling 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

每隔一段时间，一家中国公司发布一个新的人工智能模型，就会引起美国人的恐慌。

这就是上周中国的 Moonshot AI 推出 Kimi K3 时发生的事情，它打破了一系列值得注意的基准。 大多数人认为，Kimi K3 可以与美国一些领先的型号相媲美，而价格却只是它的一小部分。

新模型引发了另一轮恐慌，认为中国在垄断人工智能市场的竞赛中正在逼近美国，并指责中国人在 Anthropic、OpenAI 和谷歌已经完成的工作的基础上训练他们的模型。 （本月早些时候，《商业内幕》的阿里·巴尔强调了这一指控的讽刺意味。）

紧张局势逐渐归结为两国之间明显的战略分歧：中国接受开源或开放权重模型，允许开发人员检查和定制它们，而美国则大多保持封闭。

关于开放或封闭模型的争论

周末，一位 OpenAI 高管发表了对 Kimi K3 的长篇回应后，X 上爆发了一场争论。

唐纳德·特朗普总统的前人工智能高级顾问、新任 OpenAI 战略主管迪恩·鲍尔 (Dean Ball) 在 X 上写道：“考虑到潜在的风险，我个人感到惊讶的是，中国政府继续允许如此好的模型开源。”

他表示，开放权重策略将导致全面的“人工智能共产主义”，而开放模型可能是“减速主义者”，因为它们“阻止了人工智能资本支出”。

不过，真正让谈话继续下去的是：

他写道：“我猜特朗普政府在某个时候会意识到，他们最好的策略是围绕开放权重中国模型的使用创造大量监管风险。”

鲍尔表示，监管过程中制造业的恐惧、不确定性和怀疑（对于知情人士来说是“FUD”）将导致大多数美国公司避免使用开放模式。

他后来澄清说，这是他的预测，而不是建议，并且他支持开源，直到人工智能变得太危险为止，他说这将是“悲伤的一天”。

反应迅速而广泛。

为了支持美国人工智能实验室而制造监管混乱听起来很像“监管捕获”。 那时，政府机构的任务是规范行业设计规则以支持它，通常是根据业内人士自己的建议。

Anthropic 和 OpenAI 坚称，他们的模型过于强大，无法开放——这样做是危险的，因为任何人都可以在几乎不受监督的情况下为任何目的使用他们的工具。 封闭的系统为制造商提供了更大的整体控制力，包括安全性、访问权限和定价。 这些实验室警告说，来自中国的开放重量模型对国家安全及其业务构成威胁。

风险投资家戴维·萨克斯 (David Sacks) 曾担任特朗普首任人工智能和加密货币沙皇，随后于 3 月份担任总统科学技术顾问委员会联合主席。他表示，“监管不确定性武器化”是“完全不可接受的”。

“我们正处于人工智能政策的关键转折点。 领先的封闭实验室在人工智能模型收入方面已经形成双头垄断，他们希望政府消除他们的开源竞争，”他在 X 上回应 Ball 的帖子时写道。 这个“双寡头”指的是 OpenAI 和 Anthropic。 “他们已经把牌摆在桌面上了。 现在是硅谷其他地方——绝大多数仍然重视公开竞争的地方——也应该这样做的时候了。”

Sacks 的“All-In”播客联合主持人兼风投同事 Chamath Palihapitiya 同样直接：“未来是开源的，”他在 X 上写道。 “我们需要拥抱它并继续下去。”

著名软件工程师和企业家苏海尔·多西 (Suhail Doshi) 表示，美国人工智能实验室根据“人类数据来训练他们的产品，并且没有支付一分钱”。

“任何以‘蒸馏’的名义呼吁禁止开放重量模型的游说或立法都是胡说八道，”他在 X 上写道。 “这是针对美国未来创新的一场斗争。”

X 上的一位名为 Jukan 的 Citrini Research 分析师不同意 Ball 以及有关中国收购的警告，他写道，开源模式不会自动使公司占据主导地位。 他说，例如，DeepSeek 可以更高效地运营，从而降低代币成本，因为它具有专有操作，而不仅仅是其开源框架。

朱坎写道：“中国公司可能缺乏足够的计算能力来满足所有推理需求，但他们不会亏本出售或无法收回培训成本。”

阅读下一篇

Peter Gelling 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

Peter Gelling 是《商业内幕》的高级编辑兼周末分社社长。 他仍然撰写文章，内容主要涉及人工智能行业、全民基本收入、经济、竞选财务改革、地缘政治以及其他任何激发他灵感的事物。他曾担任 Quartz 的地缘政治编辑和 GlobalPost 的高级编辑。 2005年至2010年，他担任《纽约时报》驻印度尼西亚雅加达的记者。
